registers.name: PC
registers.summary: Program counter — address of the next instruction to execute.

registers.name: A
registers.summary: 8-bit accumulator. High byte of D when treated as 16-bit. Common destination for load/arithmetic ops.

registers.name: B
registers.summary: 8-bit accumulator. Low byte of D when treated as 16-bit.

registers.name: D
registers.summary: 16-bit accumulator pair — A is the high byte, B the low byte. Used by 16-bit load/store/arithmetic ops.

registers.name: X
registers.summary: Index register. Full 16-bit; works with every indexed addressing form.

registers.name: Y
registers.summary: Index register. Same capabilities as X. Accessed via page-2 ($10) prefix.

registers.name: U
registers.summary: User stack pointer. By convention used as a second index/stack distinct from S.

registers.name: S
registers.summary: Hardware stack pointer. Used by the interrupt machinery to push CPU state. In Ladybug, initialised to $1FFE.

registers.name: DP
registers.summary: Direct-page register. Supplies the high byte of every direct-page address. In Ladybug, fixed at $02 — direct-page ops access $0200-$02FF.

registers.name: CC
registers.summary: Condition-code register. Eight bits: E F H I N Z V C. See per-bit details below.

cc_bits.name: E
cc_bits.summary: Entire-state stacked — set by NMI/SWI/IRQ entry, clear by FIRQ entry. RTI inspects this to know how much to pop.

cc_bits.name: F
cc_bits.summary: FIRQ mask. When set, FIRQ is masked. Set by FIRQ entry, RESET, NMI, SWI.

cc_bits.name: H
cc_bits.summary: Half-carry. Set on carry out of bit 3 of an 8-bit add. Used by DAA (decimal-adjust) for BCD arithmetic.

cc_bits.name: I
cc_bits.summary: IRQ mask. When set, IRQ is masked. Set by IRQ entry, RESET, NMI, SWI.

cc_bits.name: N
cc_bits.summary: Negative — copy of the high bit of the result. Set if the result is negative in two's-complement.

cc_bits.name: Z
cc_bits.summary: Zero — set if the result was zero.

cc_bits.name: V
cc_bits.summary: Overflow — set on signed two's-complement overflow.

cc_bits.name: C
cc_bits.summary: Carry/borrow — set on unsigned overflow (add) or borrow (subtract).
